sessions.details: recorded 1981-06-24 for Friday Rock Show. First TX 1981-07-17

track_listing.details: 7" Single from High N Dry; Vertigo

transcript.text: 275 and 285, also in stereo on VHF. This is National Radio 1.
Well, hello there. This is TV on the Radio here, Thomas the Vance, and welcome once again to The Friday Rock Show.
Right, this evening we replay the quite recent session that we recorded with the Handsome Beasts.
And we air 20 minutes of an American radio special that featured those southern boys who are going to come over here very shortly.
They're called 38 Special.
Top of that, I've got some classic stuff from Girl School, from Asherah, and from Led Zeppelin, among others.
Some new releases by Michael Chenka, Cozy Powell, The Tubes, Riot, Legs Diamond, and Blackfoot.
The menu of what you can eat at Castle Donington tomorrow.
And the Friday Night Connection.
But we start with this.
The brand new singles that are out this week.
The one you just heard was by the Michael Chenka group.
It's called Ready to Rock.
And before that, you heard the new single by Def Leppard.
That title is Let It Go.
It's 11 and a half minutes or thereabouts after 10 o'clock,
and I'd like to dedicate both of those records to all the Boxford Headbangers,
from a gentleman by the name of Jake, who sent me a very nice picture of the Hard Raw Score and Force,
which is a waterfall.
Also, the Michael Schenker Group were desperately requested by the Airville Headbangers,
and Lee James, with best wishes from, I think it's Gary the Whitesnake Loon Romsey,
who lives in Fritzing Hall in Bradford Nine.
Nice little card here from a lady by the name of Wendy.
She is in Cornwall at the moment, actually in Mousel.
She wants me to say hello to her big sister Mel and also Paul and Deb and Steve and Dave
and anyone else sharing, and everybody else rather, sharing the caravan down there in Linstep Haven.
Also wants to be remembered to her friends Kathy and Lorraine and Noddy
and Ruth and Brian and Kate and Christy and everybody else that knows that good lady.
Oh, and by the way, a fellow by the name of Malcolm says hello to Mum and Dad and Nigel
at 29 Greenfield Mount Wakefield in Yorkshire
and also to Jan and Steve and Baby Robert
who are currently ensconced happily, I hope, in Scarborough in Yorkshire.
That was Cozy Powell and Friends, a new single called Sooner or Later.
It's the new single with vocals from Cozy Powell and his mates
and it's released today.
It's also going to be taken from a new album that I received about an hour ago on cassette
and I've been listening to it in the car and it's really good.
The album's titled Tilt.
His friends on this particular venture are
elma gantry on vocals david sanchez on synthesizer don airy on keyboards gary moore on guitar
jack bruce plays some of the bass so does neil murray out of whitesnake the remarkable mr jeff
beck is also featured on the album and before that you heard a single that's just been re-released
it was the first ever single put together by girls school it was released about two and a
half years ago it hasn't been available for about two years and if you fancy it you'll find it on
the city record label it's called take it all away it's 20 minutes after 10 on the friday rock
show here's the first of four from the repeat session by the handsome beasts that's the tubes
from their album the completion backward principle let's make some noise tonight this is exactly what
we're trying to do lots of noise tomorrow night at castle donnington good noise dedicate that
track if i may to a load of headbangers who are at the moment journeying from st cybus in scotland
down to castle donington no names but i know they're heavy because they say so have a good
journey get there safely nice little message here from louise chekhanouski louise lives in
penistone near sheffield in yorkshire and also she wants me to say hello to her stephen webb type
boyfriend i've got a feeling they're actually together on holiday somewhere at the moment
what's to be remembered to her friends
David Cook and Jonathan and Craig Marriott
especially Craig because he's great and I love him
here's a little thing
from a load of people including
Kim Hancock of Leeds
and Lindsay and Lou and Bramwell I think it is
in Holmworth
who we hope are listening tonight
Jackie Sands sends all her love
and lots of love to Adrian and Wayne Lollum
in Feltham and Middlesex
message to those two people is thanks for a fabulous holiday
Jane says hello to Debbie
and Kim and everybody else you know
and before the tubes you heard something from our session by the handsome beast that something is a
track called local heroes which incidentally is going to be on a compilation album coming out in
september actually on the 4th of september the compilation album will be called heavy metal
heroes all the north american bands that band was called legs diamond from an album that came out in
the states called legs diamond in 1977 the track was called stage fright i'm playing it from an
import i don't think it's been issued in this country and i played especially for trace and
janine and windy and dave who are traveling at the moment from western zoiland which is down in
zommerzett toward donnington they want to be remembered to pete and dave and andy whom they
all met on the coach going to see the pink floyd and all the guys in the band called cinderella
who they met up with at the rainbow gig at hammersmith the trace janine windy and dave
if you're traveling to all donnington as you four are and indeed to everybody who's going in
that general direction ease on the accelerator there's plenty of time prior to that something
from the band called riot it's a track from their new album that's just come out in this country on
electra the title of the album is fire down under the track was outlaw and that was for maurice
maurice mitchison who lives in wincanton tyne and weir who writes quite frequently hope you enjoyed
it maurice here's something calm and spacey and delightful by ashra a handsome beast from our
session by them that was called another day it's going to come out on an album sometime this
autumn the album is going to be called bestiality and prior to that something from the album
correlations by ashra a band who come out of germany the track was called fantasus and it
was demanded by stephen lloyd of 66 gibbsfold road hartcliffe in bristol and he wanted it
for himself and his wife pauline and also their baby son stephen and their friends max and carrie
poncho riley and ian i'm trying to lose wade lewis time for the friday night connection the tracks i
played last week were these belgian tom's hat trick get somebody to tell you the story behind
that title one day i can't broadcast it it was by white snake it's from the album called trouble
then i played ac dc from highway to hell shot down in flames from the album agents of fortune
blue oyster cult and don't fear the reaper to the best of my recollection which as you well
though, isn't that good? Virtually everybody got it right. Whitesnake, ACDC, Blue Oyster
Cult, Donington, tomorrow. As ever, though, there can only be one winner. Now, don't drive
off the road when I tell you who it is, because I'm told by this letter that en route from
Gallashields in Scotland, down to Donington, is Steve Cowan. Easy, easy on that steering
wheel. Steve, you've got yourself a £10 record token. You are the lucky fella who got his
letter dragged out of the bag so the 10 pound record token should be with you shortly the music
of course will be with you by white snake acdc and blue oyster cult tomorrow mr cowan has put
together a friday night connection three bits of music to get your brain cells popping
Watch the flowers go to bed, you're scared never have to breathe, you've got tattoos to leave, feel all the pain, fingertips and holy, sail away, immortality falls, like me, feel all the pain.

What do you like connection? Put together by Steve Cowan, who normally lives at 219 Magdala Terrace, Gallow Shields in Scotland, but at the moment is en route for Donington, Castle Donington, in a little minibus.
To all his mates.
It is now a £10 record token richer.
Wise choice of music, Steve.
Name of the game is this, if you think you know what the connection is, and there definitely is a connection between those three bits of music.
write it down on a postcard, letter card, any form of communication you care to utilise
and send it to The Friday Night Connection, The Friday Rock Show, BBC Radio 1, London, W1A4WW.
And may I wish you the best of luck.
And also I'd like to thank everybody who wrote in for The Friday Night Connection last week
because there were cards and letters and communications galore.
Only one one of those always, but thanks for joining in.
it's 11 11 and a half i know what they want me to do they pick something real saw them last sunday
in nuremberg i'm gonna see him again tomorrow at castle donington i really do rate them and they're
very nice gazers blackfoot that was and rattlesnake rock and roller which is from the latest album by
blackfoot which is out on atco in this country the album is called a marauder 90 minutes after
11 i've just heard some really tasty news from our duty officer the person that takes the
personal messages at the bbc here i'm just told by a gentleman who's called garth who lives up
there in brig in south humberside that lorraine and chris have actually got engaged while listening
to this program i think that's wonderful congratulations to both of you i wish you the
well just happiness everything goes well for you during the rest of your engagement now don't break
it off before the end of the show sue coombs also phoned in thank you sue for the nice things you
said about the music. Sue lives down there in Hillhead, near Portsmouth. Talk a little here
about Donington. I'm told that the food available on the site tomorrow at the Monsters of Rock
Festival will be as follows. Chicken and chips and pizza and donut kebabs and fish and chips
and ice cream. Hot potatoes, Chinese food, China, what? Chinese food. Nearly said it in Chinese.
Shell fish, sandwiches and rolls, cold drinks, German sausages, Indian food. That's nice.
Pancakes, homemade sweets, tea and coffee, beef burgers, African food and waffles.
And under this, I'm reading a press release here, under the heading of Loads of Loos Department,
and due to the heavy emphasis on Indian food and the prune juice bar,
the promoters have thoughtfully purchased over 3,000 loo rolls.
These are the extra long version, each one approximately one mile.
By our reckoning, they will put an end to those, well, who might feel, well, you know.
And if you put the whole thing end to end, all the toilet rolls, this is,
they would reach from Castle Donington to New York.
Also, they're providing more toilets than the Public Health Act requires.
On the bill tomorrow at Donington, the whole thing starts at one o'clock.
The gates open at 10.30 are Moore, Blackfoot, Slade, Blue Oyster Cult, Whitesnake and ACDC.
Please, though, do remember, as at most festivals, certainly no cameras.
Don't take glass bottles in. No tape recorders either.
and let's keep it a whole day of wonderful music and no trouble.
Now I'm going to play you something which is really tasty.
